narratives.Description: Port scripts/triage_summary.py to a TypeScript triage summary module, preserving the triage-cache one-line headline and the in-flight count plus scope-gap wording the welcome surface emits. This story is independently buildable in its own packages/core/src/triage/summary subdirectory and ships a golden parity binary against the Python oracle.
narratives.ImplementationPlan:
1. Create the packages/core/src/triage/summary module that ports the headline composition, the filesystem-truth in-flight count, and the scope-gap line from triage_summary.py, with vitest unit tests in the module directory.
2. Add a packages/cli/src/triage-summary-parity.ts parity binary that runs the TS summary and the Python scripts/triage_summary.py oracle and asserts byte-identical output with cache off.
3. Verify the port with the new vitest tests and the parity binary while the Python module remains the oracle.
narratives.UserStory: As an operator starting a session, I want triage:summary available in TypeScript, so that the one-line triage headline and in-flight count come from the TS engine with the same wording as the Python module.

narrative.Acceptance: Running triage:summary on the TS port renders the same one-line headline text the Python module emits for an identical cache.
The TS summary returns the in-flight count from a live count of running active vBRIEFs, matching the Python filesystem-truth count.
Running the triage-summary parity binary returns exit 0 and renders byte-identical output to the Python triage_summary.py oracle with cache off.

expected_outputs: New packages/core/src/triage/summary module with vitest unit tests
Byte-identical parity vs scripts/triage_summary.py under cache-off
swarm.missing_traces_justification: #1530 Wave-3 framework-internal TS port traced to GitHub issue #1725 and umbrella epic #1530; no specification.vbrief.json spec-section applies to maintainer tooling.

references.title: Issue #1725: feat(ts-migration): port the triage:* family to TS (Wave 3)
